business.headline: Airlines raise fares as Iran conflict sends jet fuel prices soaringAustralia's Qantas Airways, Scandinavian airline SAS and Air New Zealand announced fare increases on Tuesday, linking the move to the sharp rise in fuel prices caused by the Middle East conflict.

Government says LPG production up 10% today, dismisses shortage concernsGovernment sources said oil refineries have increased LPG production by around 10% following directions issued earlier and that additional administrative measures have been put in place to prevent hoarding and malpractice.

Middle East countries cut daily oil output amid Hormuz disruptionFour of the region's largest producers, including Saudi Arabia, Iraq, the United Arab Emirates and Kuwait have collectively reduced output by as much as 6.7 million barrels per day.
Sensex closes 640 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; M&M gains 4%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 639.82 points to close at 78,205.98, while the NSE Nifty50 added 233.55 points to end at 24,261.60.

Essential Commodities Act invoked by India amid LPG shortage. What does it mean?Restaurants, hotels and commercial kitchens in cities such as Delhi, Mumbai and Bengaluru have reported difficulty getting commercial LPG cylinders, forcing some establishments to scale down operations or temporarily shut. The situation has pushed the government to invoke the Essential Commodities Act

Silver jumps nearly Rs 10,000: Will the Iran-US war push prices even higher?Geopolitical shocks are once again driving money into precious metals. Silver has surged nearly Rs 10,000 amid rising tensions between the US, Israel and Iran. As uncertainty deepens, the big question now is: can silver rally even further?

Prolonged Gulf conflict could be catastrophic for oil markets, says Aramco chiefSpeaking during a media call after the company released its 2025 financial results, Saudi Aramco CEO Amin Nasser said the disruption represents the most serious crisis faced by the region's oil and gas sector in recent years.

EPF rate stays at 8.25%: Should investors continue with VPF or choose SIPs?For many salaried employees, EPF and Voluntary Provident Fund (VPF) are trusted tools for retirement savings. But with the EPF rate staying at 8.25%, some investors are wondering if they should keep contributing to VPF or move extra savings into SIPs for better long-term returns?

Live: LPG output up 10%, no shortage in India: Govt sources clarifyLPG Shortage Live Updates: A growing LPG cylinder shortage is disrupting restaurants and hotels across several Indian cities, raising concerns over a widening gas supply crisis. Hospitality associations have warned that eateries are struggling to procure commercial LPG cylinders, forcing some establishments to shut operations temporarily. The shortage is being reported in cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru and Kolkata, where restaurant bodies say supplies have become irregular over the past few days. The government has asked oil companies and refineries to increase LPG production to stabilise supply. Track this live blog for the latest updates on the LPG cylinder shortage, city-wise impact on restaurants, supply disruptions, and steps being taken to address the crisis.

When crude oil hit a record $147 per barrel in 2008 and the reason was not warCrude oil briefly touched $120 amid Gulf tensions, reviving memories of the historic 2008 oil shock when prices hit $147. But unlike this conflict, the 2008 surge was not driven by war or geopolitics. So what pushed oil prices so high? Read on to find out.

Why have crude oil prices fallen below $100, and will it go up again?Crude oil prices had surged above $100 per barrel on Monday but fell today after recent comments from US President Donald Trump. Experts say that even though prices have fallen today, the risk to global oil supply has not gone away.

LPG shortage shuts 20% of Mumbai hotels and restaurants Live UpdatesThe association warned that up to 50% of hotels in the city could shut down within the next two days if the gas supply situation does not improve.
Sensex jumps 500 points, Nifty above 24,100; IndiGo up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex added 379.79 points to 77,945.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 106.40 points to 24,134.45 as of 9:28 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market crash again or open higher today?Markets have been under pressure in recent days due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Last week, both benchmark indices, the Sensex and the Nifty, recorded their worst weekly performance in more than a year.

Why oil controls the world economyOil has shaped wars, alliances and global economies for more than a century. A sudden disruption in supply can shake financial markets, push up inflation and send governments scrambling for solutions.

Trump sons-backed Aureus to merge with drone firm Powerus targeting Pentagon salesDrones have become a major procurement priority for the Pentagon and are widely used in Ukraine, where dense air defense systems near the front lines limit the deployment of conventional aircraft.

Stock markets sink worldwide as oil prices briefly spike close to $120 per barrelThe S&P 500 fell 1.3 per cent, coming off its worst week since October. The Dow Jones Industrial Average was down 721 points, or 1.5 per cent, as of 9:35 am Eastern time, and the Nasdaq composite was 1.2 per cent lower. That followed even worse losses in European and Asian stock markets.

G7 nations hold off on emergency oil stock release as Gulf crisis lifts pricesThe bloc of the world's seven most powerful economies said it is prepared to act to stabilise oil markets if needed but believes the situation has not yet reached a stage requiring coordinated measures such as a joint release of strategic petroleum stocks.

Keep your PPF, SSY, NPS active: Complete this before March 31 deadlineThe March 31 deadline is almost here! Ensure your PPF, SSY, and NPS contributions are up-to-date to avoid penalties and keep your accounts running smoothly.

Why Japan holds the world's third-largest strategic oil reserveJapan, one of the world's largest oil importers, may release oil from national reserves as Middle East tensions threaten supply. Why is the country considering this move, and why does it rely so heavily on imported oil? Let's have a look.

entertainment.headline: Randeep Hooda, Lin Laishram welcome baby girl: Lifetime of love Actors Randeep Hooda and Lin Laishram have welcomed their first child, a baby girl. The newborn shares her birthday with Randeep's father.

Did Timothee Chalamet's opera remark derail his Oscar dream? Controversy explained Timothee Chalamet, once considered a leading contender for Best Actor at this year's Oscars, now finds his prospects clouded by controversy over comments about ballet and opera. The actor has been nominated for his role in Marty Supreme.

Anupam Kher joins Prabhas in Fauzi, a 1940s drama on Azad Hind Force Veteran actor Anupam Kher has officially joined the cast of Prabhas-led Fauzi. The film, directed by Hanu Raghavapudi, is set against the backdrop of the 1940s Azad Hind Force.

Explained: Why BTS creator and HYBE boss Bang Si-hyuk faces trading violation probe Bang Si-hyuk, chairman of South Korean entertainment giant HYBE and mastermind behind the K-pop superband BTS, has been named to Billboard's 2026 Global Power Players list. Here's looking at the reasons he faces investigation over alleged unfair trading practices under the Capital Markets Act.

Why is Quentin Tarantino upset with Rosanna Arquette? Read here Director Quentin Tarantino reacted to actor Rosanna Arquette's remarks, calling the use of the N-word in his films racist. He criticised Arquette for biting the hand that fed her.

Bombay High Court protects Shilpa Shetty's personality rights The Bombay High Court has issued a key order protecting Shilpa Shetty's personality rights and addressing the misuse of her identity online. Her lawyer says the ruling sends a strong message about deepfakes and digital impersonation.

Kritika Kamra-Gaurav Kapur ditch reception for quirky after-party. See invite Kritika Kamra and Gaurav Kapur are set to marry in Mumbai on March 11 and 12. With just a day left for the big day, the couple's quirky after-party invitation card has caught the attention of all.

Hollywood to Oscar stage, Priyanka Chopra's westside story keeps getting better From starring in Quantico, Citadel and The Bluff on screen to presenting at the Golden Globes and the Oscars on stage, Priyanka Chopra has arrived in Hollywood and how! We have a look at her steady journey in the world of western showbiz so far.

Anupam Kher attends Satish Kaushik's daughter's school play, recalls his legacy Anupam Kher recently attended a school play to support the late Satish Kaushik's daughter, Vanshika. Kher shared his joy on social media and praised Vanshika's talent, while also reflecting on old memories with Kaushik.

Rihanna in shock after LA home shooting; adds new security measures. Photos After the terrifying shooting at her Los Angeles mansion, Rihanna is prioritising her family's safety by implementing strict new security measures while trying to process the targeted attack.

Actor Bellamkonda Sai Sreenivas introduces special person, clarifies engagement buzz Telugu actor Bellamkonda Sai Sreenivas publicly introduced Kavya Reddy by sharing pictures from a private family event. The Telugu actor clarified that the occasion was not an engagement and that formal celebrations will be announced later.

Aamir Khan changes earlier OTT stance, announces Sitaare Zameen Par on Sony LIV After initially speaking about avoiding an OTT release for Sitaare Zameen Par, Aamir Khan has now given in to the trend and announced the film's streaming platform.

Varun Dhawan's Hai Jawani avoids Toxic clash: 'No unnecessary competition' Varun Dhawan-starrer Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai has a new release date after the makers of Toxic postponed their film to June 5, thereby setting up a clash with the David Dhawan romantic comedy. The makers of Varun's film said they believed filmmakers should support each other rather than "compete unnecessarily".

Gold tracksuit, high kicks: Zeenat Aman shares BTS story from The Great Gambler song Did you know that Zeenat Aman was never a confident dancer? The veteran actor made this revelation with a video from her 1979 film The Great Gambler.

Fact Check: Truth behind Ranbir-Sai Pallavi's viral wedding pic from Ramayan sets A photo featuring Ranbir Kapoor and Sai Pallavi is going viral, with people claiming that it was leaked from the sets of Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayan. Here's the truth behind the widely shared photo.

Suriya's Karuppu not releasing on April 10, director RJ Balaji to share update soon Director and actor RJ Balaji has clarified that the much-anticipated film Karuppu, starring Suriya, will not release on April 10. The filmmaker revealed that an official release date will be confirmed in ten days.

Thaai Kizhavi, Tamil film anchored by 63-year-old actor, mints Rs 50 crore globally Thaai Kizhavi, led by Radikaa Sarathkumar, surpassed Rs 50 crore in worldwide box office earnings. It marks a rare industry milestone for a film without a male star succeeding and providing a boost to the Tamil film industry.

XO, Kitty 3 trailer: Lana Condor's Lara Jean returns as Kitty navigates love, school Lana Condor reprises her role as Lara Jean in the upcoming third season of XO, Kitty, joining the cast as Kitty navigates new challenges and relationships.

Mannara Chopra breaks down over Anurag Dobhal's crash, sends message to his parents Mannara Chopra shared an emotional video after YouTuber Anurag Dobhal's shocking crash livestream left fans concerned. The Bigg Boss 17 fame also made a heartfelt appeal to his family while sharing an update on his condition.

general news.headline: Government says LPG production up 10% today, dismisses shortage concernsGovernment sources said oil refineries have increased LPG production by around 10% following directions issued earlier and that additional administrative measures have been put in place to prevent hoarding and malpractice.

How Iran war is forcing restaurants in Mumbai, Bengaluru, Chennai to shut  Live UpdatesThe ripple effect of the Iran conflict and the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global energy transit route, has led to an LPG cooking gas crunch in India. It has severely impacted the hospitality sector in Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu and Karnataka.

Jaanbaz of Ramadan War: Is Mojtaba Khamenei wounded? Iran state TV drops hintAfter Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's killing in a US-Israeli strike, his son, Mojtaba Khamenei, was appointed the Supreme Leader. However, Mojtaba neither appeared in public nor delivered an address after that. The state media calling him a 'Jaanbaz of Ramadan War' has added to the buzz that he might have been injured in the strikes.

India pumps 5,000 tonne of diesel into Bangladesh amid Iran war strainAmid war-driven energy strain, India has begun pumping 5,000 tonnes of diesel to Bangladesh through the cross-border Friendship Pipeline as part of an annual agreement to supply 180,000 tonnes. The first consignment on Tuesday marks the start of the five-year-long supply plan, and could be seen as a sign of bilateral energy ties stabilising after Sheikh Hasina's exit.

BCCI announces Rs 131 crore for T20 World Cup 2026 winners. How it compares to 2024?T20 World Cup 2026: BCCI has announced Rs 131 crore for the T20 World Cup-winning side, surpassing the Rs 125 crore awarded in 2024, as India became the first team to defend the title.

Hospitality associations have warned that eateries are struggling to procure commercial LPG cylinders, forcing some establishments to shut operations temporarily. CBSE clarifies viral 'Rickroll' QR code in Class 12 maths paper, says exam security intactCBSE has clarified that the Class 12 mathematics board exam question papers are genuine and their security remains uncompromised after concerns emerged over a QR code linking to a YouTube video of 'Never Gonna Give You Up' by Rick Astley, a prank widely known online as "rickrolling".

Live: Can't run away from karma, Mahua Moitra takes jibe at Om BirlaBudget Session Live Updates: The Lok Sabha has taken up an Opposition-backed resolution seeking the removal of Speaker Om Birla, with the discussion presently in progress. The government has rejected the Opposition's demand for a separate discussion on the Middle East crisis in Parliament, stating that Jaishankar has already briefed both Houses on the matter. The second half of Parliament's Budget Session began on a turbulent note on Monday as Opposition MPs raised slogans in the Rajya Sabha while External Affairs Minister Subrahmanyam Jaishankar briefed the House on the situation in the Middle East. However, some parliamentarians are of the opinion that External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar's statement is not enough, the government must discuss the West Asia crisis and have a table talk with the opposition.

Burns till the bones: 10 weapons that are restricted or banned in warfareIsrael came under fire from Human Rights Watch for allegedly using white phosphorus weapons in a civilian area in Lebanon. The use of white phosphorous against civilians is banned under international law. Here are 10 weapons that are either restricted or outright banned in warfare.

Live: UAE intercepts Iranian missiles; blasts heard across parts of countryUS-Israel Attacks Iran Live Updates: The Middle East war entered its 11th day with a dramatic escalation over global oil supplies. Iran's Revolutionary Guards warned they would not allow "one litre of oil" to leave the region if US and Israeli strikes continue, raising fears of a wider energy shock. US President Donald Trump responded with a stark threat, saying Washington would hit Iran "twenty times harder" if it blocked tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, which carries about a fifth of the world's oil. Markets have been rattled as tankers remain stranded and producers halt pumping, while Iran's security establishment appears to be rallying behind new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei amid signs the conflict could stretch longer than expected. Follow indiatoday.in for the latest updates on the Middle East crisis.

Well played, coach sahab: Time Gautam Gambhir gets the credit he deservesT20 World Cup: Mocked, questioned and blamed for every stumble, Gautam Gambhir has quietly reshaped India's T20 identity. Trisha shouldn't have attended wedding with Vijay: Director Partibhan doubles downActor-director R Parthiban has posted an eight-minute video addressing the controversy surrounding his comments on Trisha at a recent award show. He denied any deliberate wrongdoing and commented on her public appearance with Vijay at a wedding reception.

Military said it's more fun to sink them: Trump on destroying vs capturing Iran shipsUS President Donald Trump on Monday claimed that the US Navy has sunk 46 "top-of-the-line" Iranian naval ships so far. He made this claim while addressing a gathering of congressional Republicans in Washington DC.

Family turns Lucknow train coach into 'reel studio', passenger calls helplineManmohan Verma, who was travelling from Bilaspur to Lucknow, shared a video from inside the train that showed members of the family dancing and filming reels inside the coach.

What will Balen Shah as PM mean to Nepal's roti-beti ties with India?Rapper-turned-Kathmandu mayor Balendra Shah, who once blasted India, China and the US in a viral post, is now poised to become the Prime Minister of Nepal. While the routing of pro-Beijing Communist parties points to a setback for China, what would Balen at the helm in Kathmandu mean to India?

Not a litre of oil will be exported, warns Iran as Trump threatens Hormuz takeoverTrump has threatened to take over the Strait of Hormuz, a vital transit route for global oil, if Iran attacked tankers and continued to target American and Israeli assets in the Middle East.

Can AI kill the engineering degree? Why the future of college is a real questionArtificial Intelligence is transforming engineering education, raising questions about the future of college degrees. sports.headline: Sahibzada Farhan set for ODI debut after T20 WC surge, confirms Shaheen Afridi BAN vs PAK, 1st ODI: Shaheen Shah Afridi confirmed that Sahibzada Farhan, Shamyl Hussain, and Maaz Sadaqat will make their ODI debuts against Bangladesh in the series opener on Wednesday in Mirpur.

Watch: Ishan Kishan deflects question on Kirti Azad's controversial temple remarks Ishan Kishan calmly avoided commenting on Kirti Azad's controversial remarks about taking the T20 World Cup trophy to a temple after India's victory. Instead, he urged journalists to focus on India's triumph and the team's memorable campaign.

IPL 2026 schedule for first 20 matches to be announced by March 13: BCCI The BCCI will unveil the schedule for the first 20 days of IPL 2026 by March 13, as confirmed by Secretary Devajit Sikia. The board is releasing fixtures in phases while factoring in state election dates.

Reminiscent of Viv Richards: Gavaskar's glowing verdict on Samson's World Cup heroics T20 World Cup 2026: Sunil Gavaskar compared Sanju Samson to Viv Richards and Mahela Jayawardene after his Player of the Tournament performance at the T20 World Cup, praising his rare purity of batting.

Hell of a career: Starc pays special tribute to Alyssa Healy on Australia farewell Mitchell Starc paid tribute to his wife Alyssa Healy after her international farewell, celebrating her remarkable career following Australia's Day-Night Test win over India at the WACA in Perth.

Delhi Capitals head coach Hemang Badani finds new role in The Hundred Hemang Badani has been appointed head coach of Southern Brave for the 2026 season of The Hundred, with Ian Bell serving as his assistant. The Brave will start their campaign on July 22 against Welsh Fire in Southampton.

ICC call off Doha board meetings due to West Asia chaos The ICC has called off its scheduled board and committee meetings in Doha due to escalating tensions in West Asia. Key discussions on broadcasting rights, the next FTP cycle and Olympic qualification will now be held virtually.

Jacob Bethell rewarded for T20 World Cup semis 105, named Birmingham Phoenix captain Jacob Bethell has been named captain of Birmingham Phoenix for the 2026 season of the Hundred after his standout T20 World Cup campaign, highlighted by a 105 off 48 balls against India in the semi-final.

FIFA World Cup 2026 still on track in USA despite escalating West Asia Crisis FIFA says the 2026 World Cup in the USA will go ahead despite the ongoing conflict in West Asia, with Iran's participation in the tournament remaining uncertain due to the war.

UEFA Champions League Round of 16 preview: Key clashes to look out forThe UEFA Champions League Round of 16 is set to deliver big clashes as Europe's top clubs begin the knockout battle for Budapest. Heavyweights like Real Madrid, Manchester City, PSG and Barcelona headline the ties.

Wish you were here: Rinku Singh's emotional note for father after T20 World Cup winT20 World Cup: Rinku Singh shared an emotional note for his father on social media after India won the global event on Sunday. Last month, his father passed away following a prolonged battle with stage-4 liver cancer.

Gujarat Titans rope in Matthew Hayden as batting coach for IPL 2026IPL 2026: Gujarat Titans have brought in Australia great Matthew Hayden as batting coach ahead of IPL 2026. The former opener will work with captain Shubman Gill and the Titans' powerful batting unit to sharpen their approach.

Olympic award for India ex-captain for using volleyball as a gender levellerFormer India volleyball captain Abhijit Bhattacharya is the Global Winner of the Gender Equality, Diversity and Inclusion (GEDI) Champions Award 2025 given by the International Olympic Committee. Because of his efforts, thousands of boys and girls in rural Assam now play organised sport through the community-driven Brahmaputra Volleyball League.

BCCI announces Rs 131 crore for T20 World Cup 2026 winners. How it compares to 2024?T20 World Cup 2026: BCCI has announced Rs 131 crore for the T20 World Cup-winning side, surpassing the Rs 125 crore awarded in 2024, as India became the first team to defend the title.

Coaches talked technique, but it was Dad's words that unlocked Abhishek SharmaAbhishek Sharma scored a crucial fifty in the T20 World Cup final after a string of poor performances. It was not his coaches but his father's simple words that turned his tournament around.

Neymar back in Brazil friendly squad: Big boost ahead of FIFA World CupNeymar has been included in Brazil's preliminary squad for friendlies against France and Croatia. But with Carlo Ancelotti closely monitoring his fitness, the Santos star faces a race against time to secure a place.

USA's Nathan Martin wins LA Marathon by 0.01 seconds, dramatic finish goes viralNathan Martin produced a stunning late surge to win the Los Angeles Marathon by 0.01 seconds. A viral video of rival Michael Kamau briefly running off course added drama to the race's closest finish.

The player debate was Sanju vs Ishan. The answer turned out to be bothWe spent months debating Sanju Samson or Ishan Kishan. Then, together in the T20 World Cup 2026 final, both of them put up a sensational performance against New Zealand. Seems like the team knew something we didn't.

Kuldeep Yadav set to tie the knot days after T20 World Cup victoryKuldeep Yadav will marry Vanshika on March 14 at a Mussoorie resort. The T20 World Cup winner postponed his wedding to help India claim their third title before tying the knot.

Sri Lanka cancel Afghanistan white-ball tour due to West Asia conflictSri Lanka's white-ball tour of Afghanistan in the UAE has been postponed indefinitely due to escalating conflict in West Asia. Disrupted flights, security concerns and regional tensions forced both boards to pull the plug.

Sanjuvintae Prathikaaram: Samson's turnaround like a feel-good Mollywood movieT20 World Cup final: Sanju Samson's turnaround has parallels to Fahad Faasil's 2016 movie Maheshintae Prathikaraam. Overcoming self-doubt and turmoil, Samson emerged on top as the hero for India during their T20 World Cup campaign.

Stranded West Indies, South Africa to leave India on March 10South Africa and West Indies, who were unable to return home due to a lack of flight availability, are finally set to depart for home on March 10. The teams had been stranded in India following widespread airspace closures triggered by the Israel–Iran conflict.

Stop conspiracy theories, accept India is a top side: Pakistan cricket greatsFormer Pakistan cricketers have urged their cricket community to stop conspiracy theories and accept India's white-ball dominance. India successfully defended their T20 World Cup crown, beating New Zealand in a one-sided final on Sunday, March 8.

India won a World Cup, but it doesn't feel the same anymore. Here's whyIndia's T20 World Cup win triggered an unexpected question online: why did it feel muted? The answer lies beyond cricket, in psychology, national confidence, and how success reshapes collective emotion.

By the end of IPL 2026, we will have blueprint for ODI World Cup 2027: GambhirIndia head coach Gautam Gambhir has set his eyes on the ODI World Cup 2027. Having won the T20 World Cup, Gambhir said that he will be working on the blueprint for the next ICC competition during the IPL months.

All the pain and setbacks have made me stronger: Sanju SamsonSpeaking after arriving in Thiruvananthapuram, Sanju Samson thanked his close circle and supporters for their prayers. Samson won the Player of the Tournament award after smashing three successive 80-plus scores, including an 89 in the final against New Zealand in Ahmedabad on Sunday.

Sanju Samson's my dear pondatti message melts hearts after T20 World Cup heroicsSanju Samson revealed the personal struggles behind his T20 World Cup 2026 heroics, crediting wife Charulatha's unwavering support as India's Player of the Tournament reflected on triumph, family and mental resilience.

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
